lines.en_lit: All the gates that were there did not have a mezuza except for the Gate of Nicanor, as inside the Chamber of Parhedrin,
Let us say the Rabbis and not Rabbi Yehuda, as, if Rabbi Yehuda, itself decree, and will we proceed to issue a decree to decree? Even you say Rabbi Yehuda, entire a single decree.

The Sages taught upon your gates” the gates of houses, and the gates of courtyards, and the gates of cities, and the gates of towns, all obligated the mitzva place, due to that it is stated: “And you will write them upon the doorposts of your houses and upon your gates.”

Abaye said to Rav Safra: those gates [abbulei] of Meḥoza, what is the reason the Sages did not affix a mezuza on them? said to him: Those made as reinforcement to the fort [akra] of turrets said to him: And shouldn’t the fort of turrets itself require a mezuza, since is a residence for the prison guard in As, wasn’t taught A synagogue in which there is a residence for the synagogue attendant requires a mezuza?
Rather, Abaye said: due to danger as it was taught The mezuza an individual is examined twice every seven years the public twice in a Jubilee

And Rabbi Yehuda said: an incident an examiner [artavin], who was examining mezuzot in the upper marketplace of Tzippori and a official [kasdor] found him and collected one thousand zuz from him. But didn’t Rabbi Elazar say those on the path to perform a mitzva are not harm where danger is permanent it is different, as it is written “And Samuel said: How will I go, and Saul will hear and kill me; and God said: Take in your hand a calf and say: I have come to offer a sacrifice to God”

Rav Kahana taught before Rav Yehuda: A storehouse for hay, and a cattle barn, and a woodshed, and a storehouse are exempt from mezuza, due to that the women make use of them. And what Make use? bathe Rabbi Yehuda said to him: The reason that bathe that standard are obligated But wasn’t it taught A cattle barn is exempt from mezuza,

Rather, What Make use? adorn and this is teaching: Although the women adorn themselves in them, they are exempt Rav Kahana said to him: the women adorn themselves are exempt But wasn’t it taught A cattle stable is exempt from mezuza, in which women adorn themselves is obligated in mezuza?

Rather, what have you to say, women adorn themselves is tanna’im? in my standard is also tanna’im, as it was taught of your house, your house designated to the exclusion of a storehouse for hay, and a cattle barn, and a woodshed, and a storehouse, which are exempt from mezuza, and some obligate

Actually they said: a building a bathroom, and a building a tannery [burseki], and a bathhouse, and a building immersion, which women make use are exempt from mezuza. Rav Kahana interprets according to his reasoning, and Rav Yehuda interprets according to his reasoning.

Rav Kahana interprets according to his reasoning: Your house your house that is designated your to the exclusion of a storehouse for hay, and a cattle barn, and a woodshed, and a storehouse, which are exempt from mezuza in standard And some obligate in standard. In truth they said a bathroom, and a tannery, and a bathhouse, and a immersion, which women make use; and what Make use? bathe are exempt from mezuza.

If so, this is bathhouse. teaches us the bathhouse of the public, and it teaches us the bathhouse of an individual. As it could enter your mind to say: A bathhouse of the public, whose filth is extensive, however, the bathhouse of an individual, whose filth is not extensive, I say it is obligated in mezuza teaches us

And Rav Yehuda interprets according to his reasoning, and this is teaching: Your house your house that is designated your to the exclusion of a storehouse for hay, and a cattle barn, and a woodshed, and a storehouse, which are exempt from mezuza even adorn themselves there. And some obligate in women adorn themselves However, in standard, agrees are exempt from In truth they said a bathroom, and a tannery, and a bathhouse, and a immersion, even though women adorn themselves are exempt from mezuza, because its filth is extensive.

And according to Rav Yehuda, standard, everyone agree is exempt from But wasn’t it taught And upon your gates, the gates of houses, and the gates of courtyards, and the gates of cities, and the gates of towns, and a barn, and chicken coops, and a hay storehouse, and wine storehouses, and oil storehouses, all obligated might that I include even
